# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.
# Category: cloud_database_security

resources:
- name: projects/myproject/bucket/dataflowtemp
  type: cloudstorage.v1_1.object
  properties: {}
  
variables:
 parameters: # All inputs are required for the template but not all outputs and backends support them yet so you can use these placeholders to input your data then replace it in deployment templates at runtime using variables or backend services after successful creation of resource instances (such as SQL server, storage bucket etc.)  by convention we've made a simple one-to many relationship for the sake examples
   projectId: projects/myproject # Required - GCP Project ID that owns these resources. This is where you set custom properties in your template and Cloud IAM credentials will be used to access them within our templates at runtime (i.e., when creating a managed instance with an external IP) or via the console by selecting specific instances for operations which are not listed here as they require authentication
   location: us-central1 # Required - GCP Compute Location where resources will be created, and in what order Cloud SQL starts servers there. Defaults to 'us' if omitted since all locations currently support compute zones (for example ['zones/us-east', 'zones/us-west']).
   instanceId: myinstance # Required - Unique id for your database instances on GCP and in our template it should match with the name of sql server you want to create. For SQL Server, use lower case names followed by `id` .  Defaults if omitted means 'default'. Example "test-db", or no instanceId at all
   dbName: myinstance # Required - Name for your database that GCP will assign when creating this DB in the project on our template it should match with name of sql server you want to create. For SQL Server, use lower case names followed by `id` . Defaults if omitted means 'default'. Example "test-db", or no instanceId at all
   rootPassword: CloudSQL2024! # Required - Password for the database user named defaultuser of your chosen DB system. It will not be stored in logs, log files, etc., and therefore can't recover a password by an unauthorized person if you do lose this information please set it to something long enough so that even someone with physical access could somehow obtain its value
   ipConfiguration: [] # Optional - You may want certain instances public or private. This field is array of objects, each object should have IPV4 and USERNAME fields indicating whether the instance's network interfaces are publicly accessible on Google’s load balancing service (for example ['IP_ADDRESS': 'default'])
   databaseVersion: POSTGRES_9_6 # Optional - Choose PostgreSQL version to be used. You can change this only if you have an upgrade available for your DB type and want a new release otherwise it will use default one in GCP which is Postgres 10 nowadays (DEFAULT) but supports versions upto postgres v8
   backupConfiguration: [] # Optional - If enabled then automatically creates backups every specified interval. It must be included inside the instance object, example ['type': 'daily', 'retentionPolicy' : { 'retentionDurationTerms' : 365,'totalRetentionDurationSeconds'}] with details below
   maintenanceWindow: [] # Optional - Specifies a time window in which automatic DB instances updates are applied. It must be included inside the instance object, example ['startTime': '2018-9-4T7:30Z', ‘endTime’ : “after_5pm”] with details below
   connectionName: myinstance # Optional - Custom name for your database user's current connections. If omitted defaults to a random UUID string (i.e., 'cxjrbrg12m9306kfmlwv4lqeq') but this should not be more than 57 characters as that would require an extra step on the front end
   tier: db-f1-micro # Optional - Choose a DB instance plan. Defaults to 'db-f1-micro' (DEFAULT) which is good for development, staging or small database requirements but can be changed if you have higher needs i.e., as per Google Cloud’s pricing page
   storage: 5GB_2X # Optional - Choose DB instance storage type and size at launch time in the format `type/size` (default 'INTERNAL_STORAGE') used for both data stored on disk or backup if applicable i.e., ['INNODB', "50gb"]
   CPU: 1cpu # Optional - Define how many vCPUs to use from a list of options like `2cpus`, '4cpus' etc... and its used by default as per Google Cloud’s pricing page (DEFAULT) which is good for development or small database requirements but can be changed if you have higher needs i.e., larger instances provide more resources
   allowPublicNetworkAccess: true # Optional - Allows users to access this instance from any IP address on the network using their own public SSH keys when available as part of Google Cloud’s load balancing service (DEFAULT) which is good for development or small database requirements but can be changed if you have higher needs i.e., disable allowPublicNetworkAccess, so only uses GCP's internal services and not accessible from internet
   monitoring: CLOUD_SQL # Optional - Choose the type of Monitoring provided by Google Cloud SQL Database (DEFAULT) it is good for small database requirements but can be changed if you have higher needs i.e., BigQuery or StatsD etc... – which provides more comprehensive visibility into your DB operations
